_about: Administration & configuration (AdminClusterSettings, AdminCommands, AdminIndexCommands, Configuration, ExtendedConfiguration, Setting, Logging, Log, MeteredBilling). AdminCommands endpoints are admin-only and typically enqueue background processing jobs; Configuration endpoints manage provider definitions and integration configuration.

GET /api/v1/admin/clustersettings/runtim.description: Returns the available `LoggingEnvironmentPreset` values (Development, Staging, Production, etc.). Admin-only.
Returns the configurable runtime log-level switches that overrides can target. Admin-only.

GET /api/v1/admin/clustersettings/runtim.summary: Get current runtime log level overrides
GET /api/v1/admin/clustersettings/runtim.description: Returns the active runtime log-level override configuration (preset and/or per-switch overrides) plus its expiration time. Admin-only.

POST /api/v1/admin/clustersettings/runti.description: Sets a runtime log-level override (preset, switch overrides, or both) with a positive lifetime. The override expires automatically after `Lifetime`. Returns 400 when `Lifetime` is non-positive or neither preset nor switch overrides are supplied. Admin-only.
Convenience endpoint to apply a `LoggingEnvironmentPreset` with a lifetime expressed in minutes (defaults to 60). Returns 400 for non-positive lifetimes or unknown preset values. Admin-only.
Removes the active runtime log-level override and reverts the cluster to its default configuration. Admin-only.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/remapindex.description: Publishes a `FullRemapAndReIndex` event to trigger a complete reindex across the organisation. Requires the Administration.Data RACI claim.

GET /api/v1/admin/agents.description: Returns the agents (workers) registered with the cluster, used by the admin tooling to inspect processing capacity.

GET /api/v1/admin/provider/configuration.description: Returns the raw provider configuration record from the configuration repository — typically used for debugging an integration.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/aggregate/par.description: Diagnostic endpoint that aggregates an entity's parent records. Used by support engineers to investigate entity-graph anomalies.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/archive/metri.description: Triggers archival of metrics across the organisation.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/archive/metri.summary: Admin: archive metrics for an entity
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/archive/metri.description: Triggers archival of metrics for a single entity.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/clean/userst.description: Clears cached/derived user state for the current organisation. Useful when a user account is in an inconsistent state.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/deduplicate.description: Queues an ad-hoc deduplication run for the requested entity or scope. Used for support troubleshooting outside the deduplication-projects flow.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/deduplicate/a.description: Queues an organisation-wide ad-hoc deduplication pass. Heavy operation — only used for support investigations.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/deduplicate/e.description: Queues a deduplication pass scoped to a single entity.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/externalsearc.summary: Admin: trigger external search for entity
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/externalsearc.description: Triggers an external-search/enrichment pass for the supplied entity using the configured enrichers.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/identitycount.description: Returns the configured identity counters and their state.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/identitycount.summary: Admin: get next identity counter id
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/identitycount.description: Returns the next id from an identity counter (advances the counter).

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/identitycount.summary: Admin: get unique identity counter id
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/identitycount.description: Returns a guaranteed-unique id from an identity counter.
Returns currently held id reservations on an identity counter.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/loadCsv.description: Loads a CSV file into the organisation as part of an admin import. Used for support data-loading scenarios.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.summary: Admin: create missing mesh records by entity type
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.description: Creates missing target-side mesh records for every entity of the supplied type.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.summary: Admin: create missing mesh records by entity id
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.description: Creates missing target-side mesh records for a single entity id.
Synchronises missing target-side mesh records across the organisation.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.summary: Admin: sync missing mesh records for entity
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/mesh/create/m.description: Synchronises missing target-side mesh records for a single entity id.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/PopulateOrig.description: Backfills the `OriginEntityCodes` collection on entities — used after data-model migrations.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/all.description: Queues a full reprocessing pass over all entities.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/all/e.summary: Admin: process all entities of type
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/all/e.description: Queues a reprocessing pass for every entity of the supplied entity type.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/entit.description: Queues a reprocessing pass for a single entity.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/custo.description: Re-publishes outgoing-hook events for every entity that has custom hooks defined.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/metri.description: Queues processing of a global metric across the organisation.
Queues processing of global metrics for the organisation.
Queues processing of the metrics that apply to a single entity.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/metri.summary: Admin: process metrics for all entities
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/metri.description: Queues processing of entity metrics across all entities.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/metri.summary: Admin: process metrics for entities of type
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/process/metri.description: Queues processing of entity metrics for every entity of the supplied type.
Returns an explain-log trace of the metric processing on a single entity, useful for debugging metric definitions.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/processEdges/.description: Queues a reprocessing pass over edges across all entities.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/processEdges/.summary: Admin: process edges for entity type
GET /api/v1/admin/commands/processEdges/.description: Queues a reprocessing pass over edges for entities of the supplied type.
Queues a reprocessing pass over edges for a single entity.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/reset/accoun.description: Resets cached account information for the organisation.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/resync/datas.description: Triggers a resync of all secondary data stores (graph, search, blob) from the primary data store.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/resync/datas.summary: Admin: resync entity across data stores
POST /api/v1/admin/commands/resync/datas.description: Triggers a resync of a single entity across all secondary data stores.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/send/all.description: Re-sends all entities through the processing pipeline.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/send/insight.description: Re-sends a queued insight command for an entity.

GET /api/v1/admin/commands/split/all/ent.description: Queues a split-entity pass for every entity of the supplied type. Used to reverse a bad merge across many entities.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/split/entity.description: Splits a single entity into its constituent data parts.

POST /api/v1/admin/commands/split/isolat.description: Splits one data part off an entity into its own entity, useful for diagnosing merges.

POST /api/v1/admin/memory/insert.description: Inserts an item into the admin memory store (used for ad-hoc support state).

GET /api/v1/configuration.description: Returns paged configurations (provider definitions) for the organisation. Multiple overloads exist that accept different filter shapes; consult the in-code controllers for the specific combinations supported on your deployment.

GET /api/v1/configuration/{name}.description: Returns the configuration identified by name (typically a provider definition lookup).

GET /api/v1/configuration/providers.description: Returns paged provider definitions filtered by status, name, owner, and include/exclude type lists.

parameters.includeTypes: Comma-separated list of types to include.
parameters.excludeTypes: Comma-separated list of types to exclude.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providers/coun.description: Returns the count of provider definitions matching the supplied filters (same filters as `GET /api/v1/configuration/providers`).

GET /api/v1/configuration/providers/hist.description: Returns the change history for the supplied provider definition.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providerlist.description: Returns all provider definitions visible to the caller. Lightweight alternative to `/providers` when filtering isn't needed.

GET /api/v1/configuration/jobs.description: Returns the agent jobs associated with provider configurations (typically crawl jobs).

DELETE /api/v1/configuration/deletewebho.description: Deletes a webhook registration associated with a provider configuration.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providerdefini.description: Returns the owners (users/teams) of provider definitions.

POST /api/v1/configuration/providerdefin.description: Adds an owner to a provider definition.

PUT /api/v1/configuration/providerdefini.description: Updates an owner entry for a provider definition.

DELETE /api/v1/configuration/providerdef.description: Removes an owner from a provider definition.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providefinitio.description: Returns the user account memberships on provider definitions. (Note: the spelling `providefinition` rather than `providerdefinition` is preserved from the original route definition.)

POST /api/v1/configuration/providefiniti.description: Adds a user-account membership to a provider definition.

DELETE /api/v1/configuration/providefini.description: Removes a user-account membership from a provider definition.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providefinitio.summary: List provider memberships for a user
GET /api/v1/configuration/providefinitio.description: Returns the provider definitions a specific user has access to.

GET /api/v1/configuration/providefinitio.summary: List provider memberships for current user
GET /api/v1/configuration/providefinitio.description: Returns the provider definitions the calling user has access to.

POST /api/v1/extendedconfiguration/resol.description: Resolves dynamic option lists for extended configuration UI controls (for example dropdowns whose options depend on other field values). The server iterates registered `IExtendedConfigurationProvider` implementations until one can handle the request. Returns 404 when no provider can handle the request and 400 on validation failures.

POST /api/v1/extendedconfiguration/resol.summary: Resolve a single extended configuration option
POST /api/v1/extendedconfiguration/resol.description: Resolves a single extended configuration option by value (used to render the selected option's label without re-fetching the full option list). Returns 404 when no provider can handle the request.

GET /api/v1/settings.description: Returns the user-level settings stored for the calling user. Includes the organisation and user identifiers on each entry.

POST /api/v1/settings.description: Creates a new user setting. Returns 202 Accepted when the setting has been persisted; 400 with validation details when the model is invalid.

PUT /api/v1/settings.description: Updates a user setting if a setting with the same key already exists for the caller; otherwise creates it. Returns 202 Accepted.

GET /api/v1/logs.description: Returns the most recent application logs for the organisation by reading from the RabbitMQ log stream. With no `offset`, returns the last ~1000 entries; supply `offset` to continue from a previous read. The response is `{ consoleMessages: [...] }`. Requires the OrganizationalAdmin role.

parameters.offset: Optional starting offset (inclusive). When omitted, the server reads from `lastOffset - 999`.

POST /api/v1/logging.description: Writes a log message into the server's logger with the supplied severity. The endpoint mainly exists so the UI can ship client-side errors back to the backend logger. Returns 200 with `"Logged"`. Note: any authenticated user can post; treat the logger as untrusted input.
